chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا أَخَذَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِ مِنَ الصَّبْرِ عَلَى مَا يَلْحَقُهُ فِيمَا ابْتُلِيَ بِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 105 – What (Covenant) Allah<sup>azwj</sup> has Taken upon the Momin, from the patience upon whatever he faces regarding what he has been afflicted with

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/310)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/310)
text: أَخَذَ اللَّهُ مِيثَاقَ الْمُؤْمِنِ عَلَى أَنْ لَا تُصَدَّقَ مَقَالَتُهُ وَ لَا يَنْتَصِفَ مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ وَ مَا مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ يَشْفِي نَفْسَهُ إِلَّا بِفَضِيحَتِهَا لِأَنَّ كُلَّ مُؤْمِنٍ مُلْجَمٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Isa, from Ali Bin Al numan, from Dawood Bin Farqad,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Allahazwj Took a Covenant upon the Momin (to have patience) when his words are not accepted as true and he does not avenge his enemies.  A Momin will not have any satisfaction without subjecting his soul to disgrace (before his own conscience) every Momin is harnessed (to control his worldly desires) ’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Ali ibn al-Nu’man from Dawud ibn Farqad from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Allah has made a covenant with a believer (to have patience) when his words are not accepted as true and he does not avenge his enemies. A believer will not have any satisfaction without subjecting his soul to disgrace (before his own conscience); every believer is harnessed (as to his soul in the matters of worldly desires).”
